Uzziah generally did what was right. It was good for the kingdom that a good reign lasted a long time.

This history shows Israel in confusion. Though Judah had troubles, that kingdom was happy compared to the state of Israel. The imperfections of true believers are very different from the allowed wickedness of ungodly men. This is human nature; this is what our hearts are like if left to themselves—deceitful above all things and desperately wicked. We have reason to be thankful for restraints, for being kept out of temptation, and should ask God to renew a right spirit within us.
Jotham showed great respect for the temple. If magistrates cannot do everything they would like to do to stop vice and profaneness, let them do more to support and advance piety and virtue.
